title: Washing Each Other
summary: “Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me. If I then, your Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another's feet. For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you” John 13:8, 14-15. Just think about your feet for a moment. Consider trying to move your body without the aid of your feet. Neither our head, hands, knees or back can substitute for our feet when it comes to moving the entire body effectively. The head, hands, thighs etc. don’t seem to do much of use for the feet, so to speak, as the feet do in carrying all of the other members. Think now about a day or time when you walked around for several hours. Do you remember the ache you felt in your feet? There is a perfectly good reason shopping malls have cafés, sitting areas and benches spread around – they are meant for our aching feet. Let us take a moment to appreciate the job our feet do now and then! No wonder there are some who treat their feet to a well-deserved foot massage or foot bath. They work very hard for the rest of the body. Once we highlight the role feet play in our everyday, it becomes easier to appreciate the biblical culture of washing the feet of honored guests and travelers.
